headline: Morgan Stanley and BofA Expected to Post Higher Profits After Big Banks’ Mixed Results
summary: Investors are set to hear from Bank of America and Morgan Stanley on Wednesday after a string of big banks’ first-quarter earnings reports reflected solid U.S. consumer health.  Results from JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo showed that measures of consumer credit, such as net charge-offs and delinquencies, were steady in the first quarter, even as borrowers faced higher energy prices prompted by the U.S.-Iran war.  Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

headline: Stocks Are Back to Pre-Iran War Levels. Earnings Will Dictate Where They Go Next.
summary: Global investors are growing cautious on stocks, and the hit the world economy is likely to take from the U.S. war with Iran, but not to the point where they’re ready to bet against the market, which is near all-time highs heading into the start of second quarter.  Bank of America’s  benchmark Fund Manager Survey, published monthly, suggests the world’s biggest investors are expecting the fastest inflation pressures in five years, the slowest GDP growth in four and elevated oil prices for the remainder of 2026.

headline: How The Tesla (TSLA) Narrative Is Shifting With AI Ambitions And Mixed Analyst Targets
summary: Tesla’s updated analyst narrative now incorporates a slightly lower core fair value estimate, moving from US$421.27 to US$415.30. This reflects a modest reset in price targets. That change mirrors what you are seeing across Street research, where some firms are lifting targets and others are trimming them as they reassess execution risks, capital needs and how much weight to give potential AI and energy upside. Read on to see what is driving these views and how you can keep track of the story...

headline: Investors Aren’t Ruling Out a 2026 Fed Cut
summary: Investors are no longer seeing a Fed cut this year as a long shot.  To be sure, the consensus is that the Federal Reserve is much likelier to hold rates steady as officials grapple with an energy shock induced by the war in Iran that has already caused inflation to jump.  Global investor sentiment reached its most bearish level since last June.

raw.headline: Investors Aren’t Ruling Out a 2026 Fed Cut

Global investor sentiment reached its most bearish level since last June.

headline: Masters Weekend: 25,000 Kids Sign Up for Bank of America's "Golf with Us," Back-to-Back Champion Rory McIlroy Inspires Next Generation of Golfers
summary: More than 25,000 kids signed up for Bank of America's Golf with Us over Masters weekend, a 160% increase compared to the same period last year and a fast start toward the bank's goal of 250,000 total sign ups in two years. The deadline for kids to enroll in Golf with Us is June 15, 2026.

headline: Investors Brace for Economic Growth Shock, BofA Survey Shows
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Global investors grappling with the fallout from the Iran war have slashed their expectations for economic growth by the most in four years, according to the latest Bank of America Corp. survey of fund managers.Expectations for inflation, meanwhile are at an almost five-year high, BofA strategists led by Michael Hartnett wrote in a note. Overall sentiment among investors is the most bearish since last June.The Middle East conflict, which began Feb. 28, has triggered a spike in oil
